title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. ericoides
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. ericoides, also known as grey santolina, is a type of shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its grey-green, fern-like foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of grey santolina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various ailments. Grey santolina can be prepared in various ways, including as a dried herb, tea, or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
